# Questions in Livestream 74

Live Stream #74: Who Are You? What Do You Want?

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=416" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:56</a> | Are there books on the view that Asherah was Yahweh's consort, and is she Jeremiah's queen of heaven? | He distinguished the queen of heaven from Asherah and said biblical references may point at a cultic object or practice as much as a goddess. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=764" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:44</a> | What does he most wish more people knew about Malachi? | He emphasized the book's call to orthopraxy: proper worship, proper sacrifice, covenant faithfulness, and not trifling with God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=1089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:09</a> | What would Egyptian religion have looked like in Joseph's day, and would Joseph as vizier have changed it? | He said Joseph likely entered a Hyksos-period Delta setting already full of Semitic cults and probably practiced his own faith privately rather than reshaping state religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=1373" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:53</a> | How much first-century Roman history do we really have, and can later historians like Cassius Dio still be reliable? | He said we have a great deal, and later writers could draw on earlier historians, public records, and Roman library archives, though reliability varies by historian and case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=1685" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:05</a> | If Christ is equal to the Father, why does he pray to the Father? | He appealed to Christ's kenosis and human ministry, saying prayer reflects both Christ's incarnate condition and a model given for believers to overhear and learn from. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=2000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:20</a> | What are his thoughts on John 7:53-8:11 being in the Bible? | He said New Testament text critics generally agree the passage is not original to the earliest manuscripts; it may preserve a later story, but he would not treat it as inspired text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=2267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:47</a> | What is the relation between Sheol, Abaddon, and Shachat? | He described Sheol as the grave or realm of the dead, Abaddon as destruction or the bad side of Sheol, and Shachat as corruption connected with death; they overlap but are not identical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=2790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:30</a> | Can archaeology support a 2448 AM / 1313 BC Exodus date being 420-428 years from Abraham and Hammurabi? | He said the low Babylonian chronology can roughly fit the Hammurabi comparison, but he is cautious because AM dating is late, religiously framed, and unreliable when pressed too far. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=3225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:45</a> | Since Mary was related to Elizabeth, does that make Mary or Jesus Levitical as well as Judahite? | No. He said tribal affiliation for women followed marriage, so Elizabeth's Levite status does not make Mary or Jesus both Levite and Judahite. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=3418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:58</a> | Is Hebrews 2:13 quoting Isaiah 8:18, and if so is this midrash? | He said yes, Hebrews is using Isaiah 8:18, but he reads it as a messianic use of the Isaiah 7-9 context rather than midrash. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=4015" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:55</a> | Was Zoroastrianism ever a serious competitor to Judaism, and is there evidence Jews apostatized to it? | He said some Jews did apostatize under Persian rule, but Zoroastrianism was an ethnic Persian religion and not a broad long-term competitor to Judaism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=4266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:06</a> | What was the purpose behind writing the Talmud? | He said it preserved oral tradition after the Roman crises and diaspora pressures; it aimed to be comprehensive rather than consistent, so it is useful for Jewish thought but weak as straightforward history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=4548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:48</a> | What martial arts styles has he studied? | He said he dabbled in Shotokan karate and Kendo, studied Danzan Ryu Jujitsu deeply, and later practiced Chen-style Tai Chi. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=4921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:01</a> | Is there evidence of Semitic migration to the Americas, and is Christianity weakened if God did not know or care about the Americas? | He said there is no evidence before later European contact, but the objection fails because Christianity's salvation history extends from Israel to the whole world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=5157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:57</a> | Did ancient Israel contribute anything to philosophy or science like Egypt, Babylon, Assyria, and Greece did? | He said not much survives in those fields; Israel's major contribution was religious and wisdom tradition rather than pure science or philosophy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=5397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:57</a> | What does he think about identifying Moses with Irsu and dating the Exodus to 1186 BC under Setnakhte? | He rejected it, citing problems with the Merneptah Stele, conquest archaeology, Avaris chronology, and the lack of a viable Semitic Delta population at that date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=5747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:47</a> | Were Celts in Ptolemaic Egypt like Mamluk slaves, and was King Tut mostly European? | He said Celts could appear in later Ptolemaic or Roman contexts, but King Tut was not mostly European; modern ancient-DNA labels must be handled cautiously because gene distributions move over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=6161" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:41</a> | Do evangelicals fail at symbolism, and what are the historical reasons? | He said sometimes yes, tracing it to the modernist-fundamentalist debate and a defensive turn toward naive literalism, while arguing for responsible historical-grammatical interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=6618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:18</a> | Why did Esau just happen to be headed in Jacob's direction as Jacob returned in Genesis 32? | He said the text does not state Esau's motive, but Genesis 33 suggests reunion and a display of success, with the 400 men also serving as a warning if Jacob acted badly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=7435" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:55</a> | Is getting buzzed sinful? | After clarifying the question meant alcohol, he said occasional intoxication is not inherently sinful, but harmful behavior, habitual drunkenness, or neglect of responsibilities can make it sinful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=8263" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:43</a> | Is drunkenness a matter of overindulgence or gluttony? | He said no. In an ancient knife-edge agricultural setting, gluttony meant consuming another person's ration and could be akin to killing; drunkenness is a separate issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=8506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:46</a> | What is the difference between monolatry, henotheism, and monarchic monotheism? | He defined monolatry as worshiping one god, henotheism as acknowledging many equal gods while being loyal to one, and monarchic monotheism as one true God ruling over lesser powers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=8713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:13</a> | Did Upper Egypt trade with Lower Egypt during the Hyksos period, and would Joseph's famine have affected Upper Egypt? | He said there was interaction, tribute, and likely trade; a low-Nile famine would affect both Upper and Lower Egypt, and Thebes may have relied on Hyksos grain surplus, though the extent is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=9004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:04</a> | Are the Apocrypha or deuterocanonical books inspired scripture, and was the Septuagint considered Jewish Scripture? | He said the Apocrypha are useful but not inspired Scripture because they were not Jewish canon and have disqualifying issues; the Septuagint was a Greek translation and broader collection, not proof of canon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=9591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:51</a> | What are his thoughts on the Euphrates drying up and the Temple Institute gaining five red heifers? | He did not treat the Euphrates drought as significant and said red heifers are useless for sacrifice without access to the Temple site, calling the news likely publicity and fundraising. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=10078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:58</a> | Would ancient Egyptians, Greeks, or Norse pagans recognize modern neopaganism? | Probably not. He described neopaganisms as modern reconstructions shaped by contemporary sensibilities rather than direct continuations of ancient practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=10418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:38</a> | How does salvation work for those who have not heard the gospel? | He said this is debated; people are judged for rejecting God rather than mere ignorance, second-chance views are possible but uncertain, and the missionary imperative remains. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=10940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:20</a> | Would a Mr. Empty Conjecture show only have Mr. EC on screen? | He said they had not decided yet and were still thinking through the format. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=11030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:50</a> | What is his opinion of Biblical Archaeology Review magazine? | He said it has published excellent scholars and can be accessible for lay readers, but its advertising and sensational topics require discernment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=11177" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:17</a> | How reliable is petrographic investigation of the Amarna tablets? | After clarification, he said he had not seen specific clay-origin reports, many tablets self-identify their place of origin, and most Amarna tablets were dried clay rather than deliberately fired. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=11426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:26</a> | What is his best example of the Trinity in the Old Testament? | He pointed to Isaiah 44:6 as the strongest Old Testament passage because it identifies two Yahweh figures, though the fuller doctrine requires wider biblical support. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=11857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:37</a> | Can he play some riffs with his guitar? | No. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=11919" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:39</a> | Who would be the first victim of Mr. EC's new show? | He said there probably would not be one victim; the show would likely be topic-based and examine multiple speculative theories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=12078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:18</a> | Will there be another popcorn review? | Yes. He said more were coming, likely after the Titus Kennedy discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=12280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:40</a> | What kind of popcorn does he prefer? | Plain popcorn with salt and lots of butter, though he sometimes adds spices like garlic or oregano. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vU7_A4Cvtng?t=12413" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:53</a> | Are Ham and Kemet the same, did Egypt use the name Mitzrayim, and are there books on the subject? | No. He said Ham is Noah's son, Kemet is the Egyptian name for Egypt, and Mitzrayim is a Semitic name; he suggested general Egypt references such as Egypt: Anatomy of a Civilization and Ian Shaw's Oxford History of Ancient Egypt. |
